attractions.name: Albuquerque Museum
attractions.description: Established in 1967, the Albuquerque Museum is a premier cultural institution dedicated to preserving the art of the American Southwest and the history of the Middle Rio Grande Valley. It features a diverse collection ranging from Spanish colonial artifacts to contemporary installations, serving as a vital hub for art, history, and community stories. City of Albuquerque, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Wednesday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Thursday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Friday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Saturday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Sunday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. City of Albuquerque
attractions.admission: Out-of-State Adults: $6; NM Resident Adults: $5; Seniors (65+): $4; Children (4-12): $3; Children (3 and younger): Free; Albuquerque Museum Foundation Members: Free. Free admission on Sunday mornings (9 a.m. – 1 p.m.) and the first Wednesday of every month (excluding Balloon Fiesta). Military Discount: $1 off. Free for NM SNAP enrollees (up to 5 guests). Free for Bank of America cardholders on the first full weekend of the month. City of Albuquerque
highlights: Only in Albuquerque (History Gallery) City of Albuquerque
Common Ground: Art in New Mexico City of Albuquerque
Outdoor Sculpture Garden City of Albuquerque
Casa San Ysidro: The Gutiérrez-Minge House City of Albuquerque
High Desert Ballyhoo (Aviation History) City of Albuquerque
Fred Harvey's Route 66 City of Albuquerque
attractions.tips: Take advantage of free admission on Sunday mornings or the first Wednesday of the month. Sketching and note-taking with pencils and hand-held pads are allowed in permanent galleries. Use the museum's mobile app for interactive features in the "Only in Albuquerque" history gallery. City of Albuquerque
attractions.nearby: Old Town Plaza, New Mexico Museum of Natural History & Science, Explora Science Center, American International Rattlesnake Museum, Tiguex Park. Visit Albuquerque

attractions.name: Antiquity Restaurant
attractions.description: Restaurant Antiquity is a premier fine-dining destination in Albuquerque's Historic Old Town, housed in a charming century-old adobe building. It specializes in charcoal-grilled steaks, fresh seafood, and nightly specials, offering a cozy and intimate atmosphere perfect for special occasions.
attractions.hours: Tuesday: 5:00 PM - Close, Wednesday: 5:00 PM - Close, Thursday: 5:00 PM - Close, Friday: 5:00 PM - Close, Saturday: 5:00 PM - Close, Sunday: Closed, Monday: Closed
attractions.admission: Appetizers $10-$22, Entrees $31-$48+, Chateaubriand for Two $90, Desserts (Price on menu), Wines $30-$398 per bottle
highlights: Located in a historic adobe building in the heart of Old Town
Specializes in charcoal-grilled steaks and fresh seafood
Signature Chateaubriand for Two carved table-side
Extensive wine list with over 60 selections from around the world
Intimate, candlelit atmosphere ideal for romantic dinners
Daily nightly specials including fresh fish and unique seasonal dishes
attractions.tips: Reservations are strictly required and often book up weeks in advance; it is recommended to call between 3:00 PM and 5:00 PM to secure a table. The restaurant is 'seating by reservation only,' and many locals suggest visiting in person to make a booking if you cannot reach them by phone. Try the Henry IV or the Chateaubriand for a truly iconic dining experience.
attractions.nearby: Historic Old Town Plaza, San Felipe de Neri Church, Albuquerque Museum, New Mexico Museum of Natural History and Science, Rio Grande Botanic Garden, ABQ BioPark Aquarium

attractions.name: Explora Science Center and Children's Museum of Albuquerque
attractions.description: Explora is an innovative experiential learning center that offers over 250 interactive exhibits and activities in science, technology, engineering, math, and art for visitors of all ages. The museum employs a hands-on, inquiry-based approach to learning, encouraging discovery through play and experimentation in the heart of Old Town Albuquerque (Wikipedia), Explora).
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 10:00am-5:00pm; Monday Toddler Time: 9:00am-11:00am (Explora)
attractions.days_open: Daily, except January 1, July 4, the week after Labor Day, Thanksgiving, and December 25 (Explora)
attractions.admission: Explora Members: Free, Children (under age 1): Free, Children (ages 1-11): $7, Adults (ages 12-64): $11, Seniors (age 65+), Students, or Military (with ID): $8 (Explora)
highlights: High-wire bike riding two stories high
Experiment Bar with changing monthly activities
Engineering Gravity (Ball Run) exhibit
Curious Bubbles area for creating giant soap bubbles
Grandma's Casita immersive exhibition on the science of kindness
Water Flow Patio with outdoor liquid-based experiments
attractions.tips: For families with very young children, 'Toddler Time' on Monday mornings offers a quieter environment. Adults should look for monthly 'Adult Nights' for 18+ or 21+ themed events. Most visitors spend about 2-3 hours exploring the exhibits (Explora, Visit Albuquerque).
attractions.nearby: New Mexico Museum of Natural History and Science, Albuquerque Museum, Old Town Albuquerque, Albuquerque BioPark (Zoo, Aquarium, Botanic Garden) (Explora)

attractions.name: Albuquerque Convention Center
attractions.description: The Albuquerque Convention Center is a premier multi-purpose facility located in the heart of downtown Albuquerque. Boasting over 270,000 square feet of flexible event space, it serves as a major hub for conferences, trade shows, concerts, and national athletic championships (Visit Albuquerque, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (TripAdvisor)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Hours may vary during events)
attractions.admission: Free for general building entry; individual event ticket prices vary by event (Vivid Seats, Official Website)
highlights: Houses the 2,300-seat Kiva Auditorium and a 31,000-square-foot ballroom (Official Website)
Features a state-of-the-art WSTY Mondo indoor track used for national collegiate and professional meets (New Mexico Lobos Athletics)
Offers over 167,000 square feet of total exhibit space across two connected complexes (Cvent)
LEED-certified facility with a strong focus on sustainability initiatives (Official Website)
Centrally located within walking distance of over 900 hotel rooms and local dining (Visit Albuquerque)
attractions.tips: Check the official events calendar for specific ticketed event times and door openings. Use the on-site parking garage (720 spaces) or the nearby Civic Plaza Garage for overflow. Enjoy the free public Wi-Fi available throughout the lobby areas and meeting spaces (Official Website, Cvent).
attractions.nearby: Civic Plaza, KiMo Theatre, Turquoise Museum, Telephone Museum of New Mexico, and Alvarado Transportation Center (TripAdvisor)

attractions.name: My Moms (formerly Slow Roasted Bocadillos)
attractions.description: Founded by celebrity Chef Marie Yniguez, My Moms is the successor to the acclaimed Slow Roasted Bocadillos and serves as a downtown Albuquerque staple for authentic New Mexican comfort food. The restaurant specializes in family recipes and slow-roasted meats, offering a menu that emphasizes community connection and the rich culinary heritage of the region.
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Thursday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Friday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed
attractions.admission: Free; Menu items range from ~$7.50 to ~$14.50 (My Moms Online Ordering)
highlights: Slow-roasted meats including pork carnitas, corned beef, and turkey prepared with a signature 12-hour roasting process.
Featured on Food Network\
Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
NM Hash
My Mom\'s Bowl of Love,
, "Traditional stacked Red Chile Cheese Enchiladas (Olga
s) topped with an egg.",
attractions.tips: Breakfast is served strictly until 10:45 AM, so plan to arrive early if you want their signature morning burritos. The restaurant is located inside the Modrall Sperling Building (the former Bank of America building), which can be easy to miss from the street. It is a popular weekday-only lunch spot for downtown professionals, so expect a rush during the noon hour.

attractions.description: The Historic El Rey Theater is a premier live music and special event venue located in downtown Albuquerque. Originally opened in 1941, it has served various roles including a grocery store and movie theater before becoming a dedicated concert hall and nightclub. Official Site, Off the Road New Mexico

attractions.name: Birria Estilo Zacatecas (Los Tacos Del Rey)
attractions.description: Birria Estilo Zacatecas is a popular Mexican restaurant chain in Albuquerque specializing in authentic birria and traditional street food from the Zacatecas region of Mexico. The menu features a diverse range of items including birria tacos, ramen, and pizza, as well as breakfast burritos and tortas. Known for its flavorful broth and tender meats, it has quickly expanded to multiple locations across the city, becoming a local favorite for its unique 'Taco Envenenado' and hearty portions.
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 11:00 AM - Midnight (with late-night service until 1:00 AM on select days) Birria Estilo Zacatecas Hours
attractions.admission: Free (Menu prices range from approximately $3.50 for individual tacos to $35.00 for a Birria Pizza) Birria Estilo Zacatecas Menu
highlights: Specializes in authentic Birria style from Zacatecas, including tacos, ramen, and pizza options.
Offers a unique 'Taco Envenenado' (Poisoned Taco), a regional specialty brought directly from Zacatecas.
Comprehensive breakfast menu featuring diverse burrito plates with asada, machaca, and chorizo.
Known for large-format dishes like the Birria Pizza designed for groups of two or more.
Serves traditional 'Aguas Frescas' including natural Horchata and Jamaica.
Multiple locations across Albuquerque, including Nob Hill, Wyoming Blvd, and Downtown.
attractions.tips: Don't miss the Birria Ramen for a unique fusion experience, and be sure to try the consome (broth) which is highly recommended for dipping your tacos. If you're with a group, the Birria Pizza is a standout menu item designed for sharing. Instagram Review
attractions.nearby: Nob Hill Main Street, University of New Mexico, Talin Market, Tractor Brewing Company, various Route 66 historic sites and neon arches. Visit Albuquerque

attractions.name: Bosque Brewing Company (Bosque Brewing)
attractions.description: Bosque Brewing Company is a celebrated New Mexico microbrewery renowned for its award-winning craft beers like the Scale Tipper IPA. While its physical taprooms in Albuquerque closed in late 2025 due to restructuring, the brand remains active through a major production and distribution partnership with Marble Brewery (The Paper, Bosque Brewing).
attractions.hours: Closed (Legacy Hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM - 12:00 AM; Fri 11:00 AM - 1:00 AM; Sat 10:00 AM - 1:00 AM; Sun 12:00 PM - 11:00 PM) (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Multi-award winning beers, including the prestigious Scale Tipper IPA (Wikipedia)
Strategic partnership with Marble Brewery to sustain statewide production and distribution (The Paper)
Legacy presence in Albuquerque’s historic Nob Hill district along Route 66 (Visit Albuquerque)
Active community engagement through past trivia nights, live music, and local collaborations (Bosque Brewing)
Broad retail availability of flagship canned beers throughout New Mexico (KOB.com)
attractions.tips: All official Bosque Brewing taprooms are currently closed, but you can still find their flagship beers on tap or in cans at Marble Brewery locations and various retailers across New Mexico (Bosque Brewing, City Desk ABQ).
attractions.nearby: University of New Mexico, Guild Cinema, Historic Route 66 neon signs, and eclectic Nob Hill boutiques (Visit Albuquerque)

attractions.name: Historic Nob Hill
attractions.description: Nob Hill is a vibrant district in Albuquerque, New Mexico, celebrated for its eclectic mix of locally owned shops, art galleries, and diverse dining options. Located along the historic Route 66 corridor, the area is famous for its iconic neon signs, classic architecture, and lively urban atmosphere. (Visit Albuquerque, Nob Hill Main Street)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Open 24 hours (District); Individual business hours vary typically between 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Yelp)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Albuquerque)
highlights: Historic Route 66 landmark featuring iconic neon arches and vintage signs.
Home to the Nob Hill Business Center, recognized as New Mexico's first modern suburban shopping center.
A premier destination for boutique shopping, featuring locally owned galleries and specialized retailers.
A diverse culinary hub with an array of swanky restaurants, cozy cafes, and chic nightspots.
A walkable urban neighborhood located just east of the University of New Mexico campus.
attractions.tips: Visit during the evening to see the district’s famous neon arches and signs fully illuminated. The area is highly walkable, so wearing comfortable shoes is recommended for exploring the many side-street boutiques and galleries. (Albuquerque Tijeras Mountain RV Resort)
attractions.nearby: University of New Mexico, Maxwell Museum of Anthropology, Popejoy Hall, Roosevelt Park (Visit Albuquerque)

attractions.name: Maxwell Museum of Anthropology (Official Website)
attractions.description: One of the nation's finest anthropology museums, the Maxwell Museum of Anthropology offers exhibits and programs relating to cultures around the world, with a special emphasis on the cultural heritage of the Southwest. Founded in 1932 as the first public museum in Albuquerque, it houses over 1 million objects in its world-renowned collections. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 500 University Blvd. NE, Albuquerque, NM 87131-0001 (Official Website)
attractions.region: Midtown / University of New Mexico Campus (Yelp)
attractions.hours: Tuesday: 10:00 am - 4:00 pm, Wednesday: 10:00 am - 4:00 pm, Thursday: 10:00 am - 4:00 pm, Friday: 10:00 am - 4:00 pm, Saturday: 10:00 am - 4:00 pm, Sunday: Closed, Monday: Closed (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (donations welcome) (Official Website)
highlights: *Ancestors* permanent exhibit tracing human emergence through 4 million years of evolution (Official Website)
*People of the Southwest* exhibition showcasing 12,000 years of cultural heritage and history (Official Website)
Extensive collections of Ancestral Puebloan artifacts and Mimbres pottery (Lonely Planet)
Museum Store featuring local and fair trade items and Native American art (Official Website)
Educational resources including traveling trunk programs and museum field trips (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Admission is free, but visitor donations are appreciated. Guests can obtain a free parking permit from the museum's front desk or store for the designated lot west of the building. While photography is allowed in permanent galleries, certain restrictions may apply to temporary exhibitions. (Official Website, TripAdvisor)
attractions.nearby: UNM Art Museum, Meteorite Museum, Museum of Southwestern Biology, Historic Route 66 (UNM Events)

attractions.name: Flying Star Cafe (Official Website)
attractions.description: Flying Star Cafe is a locally owned, "scratch" bakery and cafe that has been an Albuquerque institution since 1987. Known for its "fine cuisine in a coffee shop scene," it offers a diverse menu of homemade comfort food, fresh pastries, and locally roasted coffee in a relaxed, community-focused atmosphere (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Monday-Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Thursday-Saturday: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Sunday: 7:00 AM - 8:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant) (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Award-winning handmade desserts and fresh-baked pastries (Official Website).
"Petio" dog-friendly outdoor seating area with free water and treats (Official Website).
Diverse menu including breakfast, lunch, dinner, and "Gluten-Not" options (Official Website).
Locally roasted coffee and specialty tea selections (Official Website).
Community-focused atmosphere suitable for studying, dates, or family meals (Official Website).
"First Wednesday" specials with $5 slices of cake or pie from 2pm to close (Facebook).
attractions.tips: Visit on "First Wednesday" for $5 cake or pie slices after 2pm. The "Petio" is a great spot if you're traveling with a dog; they provide water and cookies for pets. Try the "Gluten-Not" desserts if you have dietary restrictions. It is also a popular spot for remote work or studying (Official Website, Facebook).

attractions.name: Balloon Fiesta Park
attractions.description: Balloon Fiesta Park is a 360-acre premier outdoor space in North Albuquerque, primarily known as the home of the world-famous Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta held every October. Year-round, the park features a Golf & Event Center, the Anderson-Abruzzo Albuquerque International Balloon Museum, and the Sid Cutter Pilots' Pavilion. It serves as a multi-use hub for recreational ballooning, youth sports, and large-scale community events like concerts and movie productions (City of Albuquerque, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: April 1 – May 31: Wed-Sun 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM (Mon-Tue Closed); June 1 – Sept 6: Wed & Sun 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Thu-Sat 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Mon-Tue Closed); Oct (Balloon Fiesta): Daily Morning (4:30 AM - 11:00 AM) and Evening (3:00 PM - 9:00 PM) sessions (No evening sessions Mon-Wed or 2nd Sun); Nov – March: Closed (City of Albuquerque, Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Seasonal (April – October); Closed November – March (City of Albuquerque)
attractions.admission: General Park Access: Free (for daily pedestrian/bike use); Golf Pitch & Putt: $6; Balloon Fiesta: $15 per session (Kids 12 & under Free); Balloon Fiesta Parking: $20 per session (City of Albuquerque, Balloon Fiesta Official Site, AAA)
attractions.phone: (505) 768-6043 (Golf & Event Center) or (505) 821-1000 (Fiesta Headquarters) (City of Albuquerque, Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
highlights: Annual host of the Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta, the world's largest ballooning event.
Home to the Anderson-Abruzzo Albuquerque International Balloon Museum.
A 360-acre Launch Field utilized for sports, recreation, and model aircraft flying.
Year-round Golf & Event Center featuring a driving range and a 6-hole short course.
Sid Cutter Pilots' Pavilion, a prime venue for weddings and graduation parties.
Direct access to the North Diversion Channel Trail for walkers and cyclists.
attractions.tips: For the Balloon Fiesta, arrive at the park by 4:00–4:30 AM for morning sessions to avoid traffic; dress in layers as temperatures rise quickly after sunrise; use the Park & Ride service for convenient access; and always check weather updates as balloon launches are weather-dependent (Balloon Fiesta Official Site, AAA).
attractions.nearby: Anderson-Abruzzo Albuquerque International Balloon Museum, Sandia Peak Aerial Tramway, Arroyo del Oso Golf Course, and the City of Rio Rancho (Visit Albuquerque, City of Albuquerque)

attractions.name: ExxonMobil Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
attractions.description: The Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta is the world's largest hot air balloon festival, featuring over 500 balloons each year. This nine-day event includes breathtaking mass ascensions, evening balloon glows, and competitive flying events that attract hundreds of thousands of visitors from around the globe (Balloon Fiesta Official Site, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 4401 Alameda Boulevard N.E., Albuquerque, NM 87113 (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
attractions.region: North Albuquerque / Balloon Fiesta Park area (Balloon Fiesta Official Site, Yelp)
attractions.hours: Morning Sessions: 5:00 AM – 10:00 AM; Evening Sessions: 4:30 PM – 9:00 PM (No evening sessions Mon, Tue, Wed, or the final Sun) (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Annual 9-day event in early October; 2026 dates: October 3–11 (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
attractions.admission: General Admission: $22.24 per session (Ages 13+), Children 12 and under Free; Park & Ride (Advanced): $20.17-$32.59; Chasers' Club: $79.17; Gondola Club: $156.79; Glamping: $2,071.54+; Camp 505: $1,036.54; Concierge: $363.79+ (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
highlights: Mass Ascension: Hundreds of balloons launching in two waves to fill the morning sky (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
Balloon Glows: Evening events where tethered balloons light up their burners in unison (Wikipedia)
Special Shape Rodeo: A showcase of unique and whimsical balloon designs (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
AfterGlow™ Fireworks Show: Spectacular pyrotechnic displays following the evening glows (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
Drone Light Shows: Intricate aerial light displays performed before dawn and in the evenings (Balloon Fiesta Official Site)
Dawn Patrol: Pilots who launch before sunrise to test wind conditions for the other balloonists (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Dress in layers as temperatures can be freezing before dawn but warm significantly by midday. Arrive at Balloon Fiesta Park by 4:30 AM for morning sessions to avoid gridlock, and consider using the Park & Ride service to bypass heavy traffic (Balloon Fiesta Official Site, Visit Albuquerque).
attractions.nearby: Anderson Abruzzo Albuquerque International Balloon Museum (at the park), Sandia Peak Tramway, Historic Old Town, ABQ BioPark, and the Rio Grande Nature Center (Visit Albuquerque, City of Albuquerque)

attractions.name: Barelas Coffee House Gil's Thrilling Blog
attractions.description: Established in 1978, Barelas Coffee House is a historic Albuquerque institution located in the city's oldest neighborhood. It is renowned for its authentic New Mexican comfort food, specifically its red and green chile and breakfast burritos, and has famously hosted visitors such as President Barack Obama. The restaurant occupies three connected adobe buildings featuring a serene central patio oasis. New Mexico Nomad, Visit Albuquerque
attractions.address: 1502 4th St SW, Albuquerque, NM 87102 Gil's Thrilling Blog
attractions.hours: Mon-Fri: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM; Sat: 7:30 AM - 2:30 PM; Sun: Closed Tripadvisor, Barelas Directory
attractions.admission: Free entry; menu prices typically range from $10–$20 Gil's Thrilling Blog, Airial Travel
highlights: Authentic New Mexican standard dishes including huevos rancheros, menudo, and carne adovada New Mexico Nomad
Famous red and green chile, often served "Christmas style" Airial Travel
Historic location in Albuquerque's oldest neighborhood on the pre-1937 alignment of Route 66 Visit Albuquerque
Unique dining space spread across three connected adobe buildings with a shaded central patio New Mexico Nomad
A long-standing community gathering spot that has hosted high-profile visitors like President Obama The White House Archives
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekend mornings to avoid long waits during the popular brunch hours. Be prepared for street parking as the on-site parking lot is very small and tight. Order your chile "Christmas style" to enjoy both the red and green varieties, and note that hash browns are typically served on the side of burritos unless requested inside. Tripadvisor, Gil's Thrilling Blog

attractions.name: National Hispanic Cultural Center
attractions.description: The National Hispanic Cultural Center is dedicated to the preservation, promotion, and advancement of Hispanic culture, arts, and humanities through a 20-acre campus. It serves as a world-class center featuring a major art museum, performing arts complex, library, and genealogy center that host hundreds of events annually. NHCC, New Mexico Department of Cultural Affairs

It winds through the largest continuous cottonwood forest in the southwest, offering a paved, car-free route for cyclists, runners, and walkers along the Rio Grande.

attractions.category: Landmark, Historic Site, Multi-use Event Space
attractions.description: The historic Albuquerque Rail Yards consist of eighteen surviving buildings erected between 1915 and 1925 that once served as a major maintenance facility for the Atchison, Topeka and Santa Fe Railway (Wikipedia)). Today, the site is a vibrant cultural hub in the heart of the city, hosting the popular Rail Yards Market and the WHEELS Museum, while also serving as a frequent filming location for major Hollywood productions (City of Albuquerque).
attractions.hours: Rail Yards Market: Sundays 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM (May–Oct); Holiday/Valentine Markets 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM. WHEELS Museum: Monday–Thursday 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM, Saturday–Sunday 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM. (Rail Yards Market, WHEELS Museum)
attractions.days_open: Sundays (Market, May-Oct); Monday-Thursday, Saturday-Sunday (WHEELS Museum) (Rail Yards Market, WHEELS Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (Rail Yards Market is free to enter; WHEELS Museum is free with suggested donations) (Rail Yards Market, WHEELS Museum)
highlights: Historic Santa Fe Railway Shops architecture and "industrial cathedrals" (City of Albuquerque)
Weekly Rail Yards Market featuring over 100 local food, art, and music vendors (ABQ Mom)
WHEELS Museum with over 5,000 artifacts related to transportation history (WHEELS Museum)
Famous filming location for movies and shows like The Avengers, Breaking Bad, and Better Call Saul (Wikipedia))
STEAM Zone and Kids' Zone offering free educational activities for families (ABQ Mom)
attractions.tips: Arrive early on Sundays as popular food vendors often sell out quickly; bring reusable bags for your market finds and a camera for the unique architecture (ABQ Mom). Parking is available on-site but can be limited during peak market hours (Waze).
attractions.nearby: ABQ BioPark Zoo, National Hispanic Cultural Center, Sidetrack Brewing Co., Turquoise Museum, South Broadway Cultural Center (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Arroyo del Oso Golf Course
attractions.description: Arroyo del Oso Golf Course is a premier 27-hole municipal facility nestled in the Bear Canyon Arroyo of Northeast Albuquerque. Established in 1965, the course features a unique topography with rolling fairways, large greens, and strategic water hazards that cater to intermediate and advanced players. In 1981, it was recognized by Golf Digest as one of the top 50 municipal golf courses in the United States. City of Albuquerque, ADOMGA
attractions.hours: Dawn to dusk daily City of Albuquerque
attractions.days_open: Open 364 days a year (closed Christmas Day) City of Albuquerque
attractions.admission: Greens Fees (Mon-Fri): Regular 18-hole $27, Senior $20, Junior $14; Afternoon $22; 9-hole $20. Greens Fees (Weekends/Holidays): Regular 18-hole $33.50, Junior $17.75; Afternoon $25.50; 9-hole $22.50. Cart rentals and equipment rentals available for additional fees. City of Albuquerque
highlights: Features 27 holes, including the original 18-hole Championship course and the 'Dam 9' addition built in 1987.
Noted for its interesting topography, including three crossings of the Bear Canyon Arroyo.
Includes practice facilities such as a grass driving range, putting greens, and a chipping area.
Home to a full-service pro shop, café, and professional golf instruction by PGA staff.
Ranked historically among the top 50 municipal golf courses in the nation by Golf Digest.
attractions.tips: Arroyo del Oso is popular and can be busy; visitors are encouraged to call ahead to ensure availability and book tee times. Afternoon rates typically begin 4.5 hours before sundown, offering a more affordable option for later play. The course is a public facility and welcomes players of various skill levels, though it is particularly well-suited for intermediate and advanced golfers. City of Albuquerque, GolfPass
attractions.nearby: Arroyo del Oso Park, Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta Park, Anderson-Abruzzo International Balloon Museum, Cliff's Amusement Park, Gruet Winery Google Search

attractions.name: Bandelier National Monument
attractions.description: Bandelier National Monument protects over 33,000 acres of ruggedly beautiful canyon and mesa country on the Pajarito Plateau. The monument preserves the homes and territory of the Ancestral Puebloans, featuring petroglyphs and dwellings carved into soft rock cliffs that date between AD 1150 and 1600. It offers a diverse landscape ranging from desert canyons to high-altitude wilderness with over 70 miles of hiking trails (NPS, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Park Trails: Dawn to Dusk daily; Visitor Center: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM daily (NPS Hours)
attractions.admission: Private Vehicle: $25.00; Motorcycle: $20.00; Individual (foot/bicycle): $15.00; Bandelier Annual Pass: $45.00; Children 15 and under: Free (NPS Fees)
highlights: Pueblo Loop Trail with accessible cliff dwellings and ladders (NPS)
Alcove House, reached by climbing four wooden ladders 140 feet above the canyon floor (Wikipedia)
Tsankawi, a detached section of the park with ancient footpaths and petroglyphs (NPS)
The Falls Trail leading to beautiful waterfalls in Frijoles Canyon (NPS)
Tyuonyi Overlook for panoramic views of the ancestral village ruins (NPS)
Large designated wilderness areas for backcountry hiking and camping (National Park Foundation)
attractions.tips: The park is cashless; bring a credit or debit card for fees. Cell service is extremely limited in the canyon, so download or print digital entrance passes before arrival. During peak season (May-October), visit before 9:00 AM or after 3:00 PM to avoid the busiest times. Wear sturdy hiking shoes and carry plenty of water, as the high altitude can be dehydrating (NPS Tips, NPS Fees)
attractions.nearby: Valles Caldera National Preserve, Bradbury Science Museum, Los Alamos History Museum, White Rock Overlook Park, Tsankawi detached unit (NPS Nearby)

attractions.name: Barelas Neighborhood
attractions.description: Barelas is one of Albuquerque's oldest and most culturally significant neighborhoods, originally established in 1662 as a separate village along the Camino Real. This historic inner-city district is known for its deep Hispanic heritage, vibrant murals, and its legacy as a key stop on the original 1926 alignment of Route 66. (New Mexico Magazine, Visit Albuquerque)
attractions.address: 4th St. SW and Barelas Rd. SW, Albuquerque, NM 87102
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Wikipedia)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Albuquerque)
highlights: National Hispanic Cultural Center: A 20-acre campus dedicated to Hispanic arts and culture. (NHCC)
Rail Yards Market: A popular seasonal Sunday market featuring local vendors, art, and music. (Visit Albuquerque)
Barelas Coffee House: A legendary local eatery famous for its New Mexican breakfast and red chile. (New Mexico Magazine)
Historic 4th Street: Part of the original Route 66 alignment, lined with vintage architecture and murals. (TheRoute-66.com)
ABQ BioPark Zoo: Located within the neighborhood's boundaries, offering a world-class wildlife experience. (City of Albuquerque)
Barelas Community Center: A historic Pueblo Revival-style building serving the community since 1942. (City of Albuquerque)
attractions.tips: Visit the Rail Yards Market on Sundays between May and October for the best local atmosphere. Don't miss the 'Mural of the Heart' at the Magnolia Service Station or the 'yerberia' history at the B. Ruppe Drugstore. (Visit Albuquerque, New Mexico Magazine)
attractions.nearby: Downtown Albuquerque, South Valley, Rio Grande Bosque, Old Town Albuquerque (Wikipedia, New Mexico Magazine)

attractions.name: Canteen Brewhouse
attractions.description: Canteen Brewhouse is Albuquerque's longest-standing brewery, having served its first craft beers in 1994 as Il Vicino Brewing Company. Over three decades later, it remains a cornerstone of the city's craft beer community, boasting more than 150 local and international awards and a lively atmosphere featuring an expansive outdoor patio and a diverse food menu (Official Website, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Sunday-Wednesday: 12:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Thursday-Saturday: 12:00 PM - 10:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Albuquerque's oldest brewery still in operation, established in 1994 (Wikipedia).
Recipient of over 150 local, national, and international awards for its craft beers (Official Website).
Live musical performances on the patio stage every Thursday and Sunday (Official Website).
Hosts weekly Brain Gang Trivia nights and community events like Run Club (Official Website).
Offers an extensive food menu featuring 'Swanky Sandwiches,' house-made chili, and artisanal salads (Official Website).
Dog-friendly and family-friendly outdoor patio environment (ABQ Mom).
attractions.tips: Arrive early for live music and trivia nights as the patio fills up quickly. Crowlers and growlers are available for purchase if you want to take your favorite brew home. Be sure to check their seasonal rotation for fan favorites like the Rubi Rojo Grapefruit IPA (Official Website, Dave's Travels).
attractions.nearby: La Cumbre Brewing Co., New Mexico Clay, Heidi’s Ice Cream, and the Albuquerque Brewery District (Official Website, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Casa de Benavidez
attractions.description: Casa de Benavidez is a beloved family-owned New Mexican restaurant that has been a North Valley tradition for over 30 years. Located in a historic nearly century-old home, it offers traditional Mexican and New Mexican cuisine in a peaceful atmosphere featuring a lush garden patio, waterfall, and koi pond. The establishment includes both a full-service dining room and a dedicated carry-out section known for its popular breakfast burritos. (Official Website, Gil's Thrilling Blog)
attractions.region: Los Ranchos de Albuquerque (North Valley) (Gil's Thrilling Blog)
attractions.hours: Restaurant Hours: Monday – Wednesday: 10:30 am – 4:00 pm; Thursday – Friday: 10:30 am – 8:00 pm; Saturday: 9:00 am – 1:00 pm; Sunday: CLOSED. Carry-Out Hours: Monday – Wednesday: 6:30 am – 7:00 pm; Thursday – Friday: 6:30 am – 8:00 pm; Saturday: 7:00 am – 1:00 pm; Sunday: CLOSED. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Lush outdoor garden patio with a waterfall and koi pond (Tripadvisor)
Famous Sopaipilla Burger™, a unique local specialty (Official Website)
Designated as a "Culinary Treasure" by the New Mexico Tourism Department ([Gil\
attractions.tips: The takeout side is a local favorite for breakfast burritos and is often faster than the dining room. Secure a table on the outdoor patio to enjoy the waterfall and garden atmosphere. Note that the restaurant is closed on Sundays and only takes reservations for parties of 10 or more. (Reddit, Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Los Poblanos Historic Inn & Organic Farm, Casa Rondeña Winery, Steel Bender Brewyard, Anderson-Abruzzo International Balloon Museum, Fourth Street's "Antique Mile" (New Mexico Magazine, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Casa San Ysidro: The Gutiérrez / Minge House
attractions.description: Casa San Ysidro is a historic house museum featuring a Territorial Period Greek Revival rancho built around 1875 and expanded with adobe rooms and a re-created 19th-century corral (NPS). It houses the comprehensive Minge Collection of New Mexican vernacular art and furnishings, offering a unique glimpse into life along El Camino Real during the 18th and 19th centuries (City of Albuquerque).
attractions.hours: June–August: Tue–Sat: 10:30 a.m., noon, and 1:30 p.m. (Guided tours); Feb–May & Sept–Nov: Tue–Fri: 9:30 a.m. and 1:30 p.m.; Sat: 10:30 a.m., noon, and 1:30 p.m. (City of Albuquerque). Free Saturdays: 10:30 a.m.–3 p.m. (City of Albuquerque).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Saturday (February through November); Closed in December and January (City of Albuquerque).
attractions.admission: Adults: $6; Seniors (65+): $5; Students (13+): $5; Children (<12): $4; Groups (5+ adults): $5 each; Free on Saturdays (except during Balloon Fiesta) and for Albuquerque Museum Foundation Members (City of Albuquerque).
highlights: Comprehensive Minge Collection of New Mexican Hispanic folk art, textiles, and furniture (Visit Albuquerque).
Territorial Period Greek Revival rancho building dating back to approximately 1875 (NPS).
Re-created 19th-century corral featuring a historic barn and cabins moved to the property (City of Albuquerque).
Designated stopping point on El Camino Real de la Tierra Adentro National Historic Trail (NPS).
Traditional adobe construction with 26-inch thick walls and rooms painted with pigments from local soil (Visit Albuquerque).
attractions.tips: Public guided tours are available at scheduled times only and should be booked in advance; however, Free Saturdays allow for self-paced exploration (City of Albuquerque). Note that the historic house has no modern heating or cooling (Visit Albuquerque).
attractions.nearby: Historic Old San Ysidro Church, Corrales Growers' Market, Corrales Bosque Preserve, Casa Vieja Brewery (Corrales Historical Society, Corrales Growers' Market).

attractions.name: Coronado Historic Site
attractions.category: Landmark, Museum, Historic Site New Mexico Historic Sites
attractions.description: Nestled along the banks of the Rio Grande, Coronado Historic Site preserves the ruins of Kuaua Pueblo, established around 1300 CE by Tiwa-speaking people. The site is world-renowned for its rare pre-contact mural art discovered in a sacred kiva and offers breathtaking views of the Sandia Mountains and the Rio Grande. New Mexico Department of Cultural Affairs, New Mexico Historic Sites
attractions.address: 485 Kuaua Rd, Bernalillo, NM 87004 New Mexico Historic Sites
attractions.hours: Wednesday–Monday: 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.; Tuesday: Closed New Mexico Historic Sites
attractions.days_open: Wednesday through Monday (Closed Tuesdays) New Mexico Historic Sites
attractions.admission: Adults: $7; Children 16 and younger: Free; NM Residents: Free first Sunday of every month; NM Seniors (60+): Free on Wednesdays New Mexico Historic Sites
highlights: Ancient Kuaua Pueblo ruins dating back to approximately 1300 CE Archaeology Southwest
Reconstructed sacred kiva featuring rare pre-contact mural paintings New Mexico Department of Cultural Affairs
Visitor Center designed by famous architect John Gaw Meem El Palacio
Interpretive trail winding through the remnants of the multi-story pueblo National Park Service
Museum housing 14 original mural segments and diverse artifacts New Mexico Department of Cultural Affairs
Picnic area with magnificent views of the Rio Grande and Sandia Mountains New Mexico Historic Sites
attractions.tips: Access to the reconstructed painted kiva is by guided tour only, typically held at 11:00 a.m. and 2:00 p.m. Photography is strictly prohibited inside the Painted Kiva and of the original murals. It is recommended to call ahead to confirm tour availability. New Mexico Historic Sites
attractions.nearby: Jemez Pueblo, Jemez Springs, Jemez Historic Site, Valles Caldera National Preserve, Bandelier National Monument, National Hispanic Cultural Center, Old Town Albuquerque, Indian Pueblo Cultural Center New Mexico Historic Sites

attractions.name: Dancing Eagle Casino
attractions.description: Dancing Eagle Casino is Western New Mexico’s premier destination for thrilling gaming and delicious dining. Featuring over 400 of the hottest new slots and the state's largest bingo games, it offers a vibrant entertainment experience complete with an RV park and travel center.
attractions.hours: Casino: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM Daily; Great American Diner: 9:00 AM – 10:00 PM Daily; Cafe 108: Sun-Thu 4 PM - 10 PM, Fri-Sat 11 AM - 10 PM
attractions.admission: Free; Bingo Buy-ins: $10 - $20 (Paper buy-ins vary by session)
highlights: Over 400 modern slot machines and generous player’s club promotions
Host of the state's largest bingo games in partnership with Route 66 Casino
Great American Diner serving hearty, homestyle cooking and local specialties
Full-service Travel Center with the lowest on-reservation fuel and tobacco pricing
On-site RV Park with full-service sites and nearby marketplace grocery store
Newly integrated Caesars Sportsbook for diverse betting options
attractions.tips: Visitors must be 21 or older to enter the casino floor, though the Great American Diner and Marketplace are accessible to all ages. Join the Ultimate Rewards player's club for exclusive dining discounts and entry into monthly cash prize drawings.
attractions.nearby: Sky City Pueblo, Route 66 Casino, McCarty’s Mission, Old Laguna Pueblo, Sky City Cultural Center & Haak’u Museum

attractions.name: Downtown Albuquerque (DowntownABQ MainStreet and Arts & Cultural District)
attractions.description: Downtown Albuquerque is the city's vibrant central business district and cultural heart, featuring a major multimillion-dollar revitalization. It serves as a bustling hub for nightlife, dining, and the arts along historic Route 66, housing landmarks like the KiMo Theatre and the Albuquerque Convention Center.
attractions.address: Central Ave & 4th St (Main Crossroads), Albuquerque, NM 87102; MainStreet Office: 700-708 1st St. N.W., Albuquerque, NM 87102
attractions.hours: District: 24/7; MainStreet Office: Typically Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Growers' Market: Sat 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM (seasonal)
attractions.days_open: Daily (MainStreet office typically Mon-Fri; events like Growers' Market on Saturdays)
attractions.admission: Free (individual attractions like the KiMo Theatre or museums may have fees, e.g., tickets from $4)
highlights: Historic KiMo Theatre, a landmark Pueblo Deco-style venue (Visit Albuquerque)
Route 66 (Central Avenue) with its iconic vintage neon glow and nightlife (Visit Albuquerque)
Downtown Growers' Market at Robinson Park, occurring Saturdays from April to November (Downtown Growers' Market)
ABQ Artwalk, a monthly community event supporting local artists and businesses (ABQ Artwalk)
Alvarado Transportation Center, a hub for rail and bus travel designed to resemble the historic Alvarado Hotel (Wikipedia)
Diverse culinary scene ranging from trendy coffee shops to award-winning breweries (Downtown ABQ MainStreet)
attractions.tips: Park in one of the several parking structures (e.g., 1 Civic Plaza) for easy walking access to Central Avenue. Visit during the first Friday of the month for the ABQ Artwalk or on Saturday mornings for the Growers' Market. The area is highly walkable, especially between 1st and 8th Streets.
attractions.nearby: Old Town Albuquerque, ABQ BioPark (Zoo, Aquarium, Botanic Garden), Indian Pueblo Cultural Center, New Mexico Museum of Natural History and Science, Tiguex Park

attractions.name: Downtown Neighborhood Historic District National Park Service
attractions.category: Landmark / Historic District City of Albuquerque
attractions.description: The Downtown Neighborhood is a historic residential area in Albuquerque, New Mexico, situated between the city's Downtown core and Old Town. Established primarily between the 1880s and 1940s, it is known for its diverse architectural styles, ranging from Victorian mansions to Spanish Pueblo Revival bungalows. The district is characterized by its lush, tree-lined streets and contains several sub-districts listed on the National Register of Historic Places, including the Fourth Ward and Eighth Street-Forrester districts. Wikipedia, Downtown Neighborhoods Association
attractions.address: Roughly bounded by Central Ave NW, Lomas Blvd NW, 19th St NW, and 4th St NW Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours Downtown Neighborhoods Association
attractions.admission: Free Downtown Neighborhoods Association
highlights: Fourth Ward Historic District (Judges' Row mansions) Wikipedia
Eighth Street-Forrester Historic District Wikipedia
Mary Fox Park Downtown Neighborhoods Association
Harwood Art Center Downtown Neighborhoods Association
Lew Wallace Elementary School (historic building) Wikipedia
West Downtown commercial corridor along Central Avenue Wikipedia
attractions.tips: The area is best explored on foot or by bicycle to appreciate the historic architecture and mature landscapes. Parking is generally available on side streets, but visitors should be respectful of the residential nature of the neighborhood. The annual Historic Home and Garden Tour in May offers a unique opportunity to view the interiors of private historic residences. Downtown Neighborhoods Association
attractions.nearby: Old Town Plaza, ABQ BioPark (Aquarium & Botanic Garden), Albuquerque Museum, KiMo Theatre Visit Albuquerque

attractions.name: Duran Central Pharmacy (New Mexican Diner) Duran Central Pharmacy
attractions.description: Duran's Central Pharmacy features a world-famous New Mexican diner that evolved from a 1940s soda fountain. It is renowned for its authentic, made-from-scratch red and green chile, as well as its iconic hand-rolled flour tortillas served in a nostalgic apothecary setting. Duran Central Pharmacy, Gil's Thrilling (And Filling) Blog
attractions.address: 1815 Central Ave NW, Albuquerque, NM 87104 Duran Central Pharmacy
attractions.hours: Saturday - Tuesday: 9:00 am - 4:00 pm; Wednesday - Friday: 9:00 am - 7:00 pm Duran Central Pharmacy
attractions.admission: Free Duran Central Pharmacy
highlights: Hand-rolled-to-order flour tortillas made fresh on a comal Duran Central Pharmacy
Award-winning red and green chile available "Christmas" style [Gil\
attractions.tips: The restaurant is located at the back of the pharmacy; you must walk through the retail aisles to reach the dining area. It is a popular local institution, so expect a wait during peak lunch hours. Be sure to try the tortillas with butter while they are piping hot. Gil's Thrilling (And Filling) Blog, Duran Central Pharmacy
attractions.nearby: Old Town Plaza, Albuquerque Museum, New Mexico Museum of Natural History and Science, Tiguex Park Gil's Thrilling (And Filling) Blog, Yelp

attractions.name: El Pinto Restaurant & Cantina El Pinto
attractions.description: Established in 1962, El Pinto Restaurant & Cantina is an iconic New Mexican establishment serving traditional recipes perfected by Josephina Chavez-Griggs. The restaurant is renowned for its expansive patio dining, flame-roasted Hatch chiles, and its own on-site 'Hen Hotel' that provides fresh, pasture-raised eggs for its dishes. El Pinto, Visit Albuquerque
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 11:00am - 9:00pm; Friday - Saturday: 11:00am - 10:00pm; Sunday: 10:30am - 9:00pm (Sunday Brunch: 10:30am - 2:00pm) El Pinto
attractions.admission: Free El Pinto
highlights: Famous for flame-roasted Hatch green chile prepared using traditional techniques ZoomInfo
Voted 'Best Patio Dining' in New Mexico with a total capacity for 900 guests Visit Albuquerque
Home to the 'World Famous' Red Chile Ribs, ranked #3 in America by the Food Network El Pinto
Features one of the largest tequila selections in Albuquerque El Pinto
Includes an on-site salsa factory and 'Hen Hotel' for farm-fresh ingredients El Pinto
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for weekends and large groups; try the signature Sunday Brunch for unique items like blue corn blueberry pancakes; the patio is pet-friendly for those traveling with animals. Absolute Real Estate, Gil's Thrilling Blog
attractions.nearby: Rio Grande Nature Center State Park, Indian Pueblo Cultural Center, Village of Los Ranchos shops, and Sandia Peak Tramway Facebook, New Mexico Tourism

attractions.name: Explora Science Center & Children's Museum (Explora)
attractions.description: Explora is an innovative experiential learning center that offers over 250 interactive exhibits and activities in science, technology, engineering, math, and art. It employs a hands-on, inquiry-based approach to learning, providing real experiences with real things that put learning in the hands of visitors of all ages (Explora).
attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 10:00am–5:00pm; Monday Toddler Time: 9:00am–11:00am (Explora)
attractions.days_open: Daily, except for January 1, July 4, the week after Labor Day, Thanksgiving, and December 25 (Explora)
attractions.admission: Adults (12-64): $11, Children (1-11): $7, Children under 1: Free, Seniors (65+): $8, Students or Military (with ID): $8, Explora Members: Free (Explora)
highlights: Over 250 interactive exhibits across 20,000 square feet of space (Wikipedia))
Laminar flow fountain and experiment bar (Wikipedia))
High-wire bike for a gravity-defying experience (Wikipedia))
X Studio, a dedicated space for teen-focused programming (Explora)
Outdoor exploration area featuring water tables and a playground (Airial Travel)
Toddler Time on Monday mornings for the youngest visitors (Explora)
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets online in advance to save time; plan for at least 2-3 hours to fully experience the exhibits; check the calendar for Adult Nights if you prefer a child-free environment (Explora, Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: New Mexico Museum of Natural History and Science, Albuquerque Museum of Art and History, Albuquerque Biopark (Zoo, Aquarium, Botanic Garden), and Historic Old Town (Explora)

attractions.name: Frontier Restaurant Frontier Restaurant
attractions.description: The Frontier Restaurant is an Albuquerque landmark and Route 66 tradition located across from the University of New Mexico since 1971. Famous for its green chile-topped burritos, fresh-squeezed orange juice, and legendary sweet rolls, it offers a spacious environment with five dining rooms filled with Western art. Frontier Restaurant, New Mexico Magazine
attractions.address: 2400 Central Ave SE, Albuquerque, NM 87106 Frontier Restaurant
attractions.region: University / Midtown Yelp, New Mexico Magazine
attractions.hours: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM Daily Frontier Restaurant, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week) Frontier Restaurant, Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Free entry (menu prices vary) Frontier Restaurant, New Mexico Magazine
highlights: Famous Frontier Sweet Rolls baked fresh daily New Mexico Magazine
Homemade flour tortillas and roasted green chile Frontier Restaurant
Extensive Western art gallery featuring portraits of John Wayne Gil's Thrilling Blog
Historic Route 66 landmark status Gil's Thrilling Blog
Five distinct dining rooms with seating for over 300 people Frontier Restaurant
Signature Breakfast Burritos included on the New Mexico Breakfast Burrito Byway Frontier Restaurant
attractions.tips: Try the famous Frontier Sweet Roll with butter. The restaurant is cafeteria-style, so you order at the counter and listen for your number to be called. It is a popular late-night study spot for UNM students. Frontier Restaurant, New Mexico Magazine
attractions.nearby: University of New Mexico (UNM), Maxwell Museum of Anthropology, Popejoy Hall, Historic Route 66 UNM Events, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Gathering of Nations Powwow
attractions.description: The Gathering of Nations Powwow is North America’s largest and most celebrated Native American festival, featuring thousands of indigenous dancers and singers from over 500 tribes. The event includes the Miss Indian World Pageant, the Indian Traders Market, Stage 49 for live music, and a Horse & Rider Regalia Parade.
attractions.region: International District / EXPO New Mexico Area
attractions.hours: Gates Open: 10:00 AM; Event ends: 11:30 PM (Friday & Saturday)
attractions.days_open: Friday and Saturday (Annual event in April)
attractions.admission: General Admission: $25 (Single Day), Two-Day Event Wristband: $45 (includes in/out privileges), School Group Rate: $8 per person. General Admission tickets do not include re-entry. Tickets required for ages 2 and up.
highlights: Over 3,000 indigenous dancers and singers representing more than 500 tribes.
The Miss Indian World Pageant, the most prestigious cultural pageant for young Native American women.
Indian Traders Market with over 400 artists, crafters, and vendors selling authentic Native American goods.
Stage 49, featuring contemporary Native American music and performances from various genres.
Horse & Rider Regalia Parade showcasing traditional dress and horsemanship.
Grand Entry ceremonies held twice daily, creating a spectacular display of color and sound.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to navigate heavy traffic and entry lines. Parking is available at Gate 8 off Louisiana Blvd; oversized vehicles should use Gate 1 off Central Ave. No weapons, drugs, or alcohol are permitted, and all guests are subject to security screening. General admission seating is first-come, first-served.
attractions.nearby: EXPO New Mexico (State Fairgrounds), Tingley Coliseum, New Mexico Veterans' Memorial, University of New Mexico, International District attractions.

attractions.name: Hotel Andaluz Albuquerque, Curio Collection by Hilton
attractions.description: A historic and meticulously restored boutique hotel in downtown Albuquerque, originally opened in 1939 by New Mexico native Conrad Hilton. The property blends Spanish Andalusian-inspired decor with modern sustainability, holding the distinction of being the city's only Gold LEED certified hotel.
attractions.hours: Hotel: Open 24 Hours; Char (Restaurant): Breakfast 7:00am - 11:00am, Lunch 11:00am - 2:00pm, Dinner 5:00pm - 10:00pm, Weekend Brunch (Sat-Sun) 11:00am - 2:00pm; G. Toti (Rooftop Bar): 5:00pm - 11:00pm Daily.
attractions.admission: Free (Guest rooms and dining vary by price)
highlights: Originally opened in 1939 as the fourth hotel built by industry icon Conrad Hilton.
Recipient of the Gold LEED certification for extensive eco-friendly practices, including solar water heating.
Features 'The Casbahs,' a series of intimate, curtained seating alcoves located within the historic lobby.
Home to 'Char,' an elevated restaurant featuring New Mexico's only Josper grill for live-fire cooking.
Boasts a stylish rooftop bar (G. Toti) with panoramic views of the Sandia Mountains and downtown skyline.
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places for its architectural and cultural significance.
attractions.tips: Request a room on a higher floor (above the 3rd) to minimize noise from the evening bar activities. Be sure to explore the lobby's unique 'Casbah' seating areas for a private and cozy retreat. The rooftop bar is highly recommended for viewing the famous Albuquerque sunsets.
attractions.nearby: Albuquerque Convention Center, KiMo Theatre, Turquoise Museum, ABQ BioPark, Old Town Plaza, Sandia Peak Tramway.

attractions.name: Indian Pueblo Cultural Center Indian Pueblo Cultural Center
attractions.description: The Indian Pueblo Cultural Center is a world-class museum and cultural center owned and operated by the 19 Indian Pueblos of New Mexico. It is dedicated to the preservation and perpetuation of Pueblo culture, history, and art through permanent and rotating exhibits, traditional dances, and educational programs. Wikipedia, Indian Pueblo Cultural Center

Collection of murals by renowned Pueblo artists Indian Pueblo Cultural Center

attractions.name: Jemez Mountain Trail National Scenic Byway
attractions.description: The Jemez Mountain Trail National Scenic Byway is a 66-mile loop that winds through some of New Mexico's most diverse landscapes, including high volcanic cliffs and lush mountain meadows. This historic route connects ancient Pueblo cultures, Spanish colonial history, and spectacular geological features like the Valles Caldera. It is a premier destination for outdoor recreation, featuring hot springs, hiking trails, and the rushing waters of the Jemez River. (Visit Albuquerque), (New Mexico Tourism)
attractions.hours: Byway: Open 24/7; Walatowa Visitor Center: Daily 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Summer), 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Winter); Jemez Historic Site: Wed-Sun 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM. (Jemez Enterprises), (New Mexico Historic Sites)
attractions.admission: Scenic Byway: Free; Jemez Historic Site: $7 adults, Free children 16 & under; Valles Caldera: Free; Bath House soaks: $12-$18. (New Mexico Tourism), (New Mexico Historic Sites), (Jemez Springs Bath House)
attractions.phone: 575-834-7235 (Walatowa Visitor Center), 575-829-3535 (Jemez Ranger District) (Jemez Pueblo), (Aztec NM)
highlights: Visit the Jemez Historic Site to see the ruins of Giusewa Pueblo and the San José de los Jémez Mission.
Witness the unique mineral formations of the Soda Dam, created by millennia of calcium carbonate deposits.
Soak in the natural mineral hot springs at the historic Jemez Springs Bath House or primitive riverside pools.
Explore the 89,000-acre Valles Caldera National Preserve, a massive collapsed volcanic caldera.
Hike the easy trail to Jemez Falls, a stunning 70-foot waterfall surrounded by Ponderosa pine.
Stop at the Walatowa Visitor Center to learn about the history and culture of the Jemez Pueblo people.
attractions.tips: Check for winter road closures on Highway 4, particularly between La Cueva and Los Alamos. Stay hydrated as the trail reaches elevations over 8,000 feet, and always respect Pueblo privacy by not photographing people or sacred areas without explicit permission. (New Mexico Tourism), (Expedia)

attractions.name: Kasha-Katuwe Tent Rocks National Monument
attractions.description: Kasha-Katuwe Tent Rocks National Monument is a remarkable outdoor laboratory on the Pajarito Plateau featuring unique cone-shaped "tent rock" formations. These hoodoos were created by volcanic eruptions 6 to 7 million years ago, leaving deposits of pumice and ash over 1,000 feet thick. Managed in cooperation with the Cochiti Pueblo, the site offers stunning slot canyons and panoramic views of the Sangre de Cristo and Jemez mountains. Bureau of Land Management, Visit Albuquerque

attractions.days_open: Thursday through Monday (Closed Tuesday and Wednesday) Bureau of Land Management
attractions.admission: Total: $25 for adults. Includes a $5 BLM Ticket Reservation (waived for America the Beautiful Pass holders) and a $20 Cochiti Pueblo Tribal Access Pass ($10 for youth ages 2-16; free for children under 2). Reservations must be made in advance via Recreation.gov. Bureau of Land Management, Recreation.gov

highlights: Unique cone-shaped "tent rock" formations and hoodoos created by volcanic activity.

attractions.tips: Reservations are mandatory via Recreation.gov and often sell out weeks in advance. Dogs, drones, and motorized vehicles are strictly prohibited. Bureau of Land Management, Road Trip Queen

attractions.description: Rio Grande Credit Union Field at Isotopes Park, affectionately known as "The Lab," is a premier minor league baseball stadium and home to the Triple-A Albuquerque Isotopes. Opened in 2003 on the footprint of the former Albuquerque Sports Stadium, it preserves the rich legacy of the Albuquerque Dukes while offering modern amenities. The park is celebrated for its family-friendly environment, 360-degree concourse, and the iconic grass hill in right field known as the Berm. MLB.com, Wikipedia

Fun Zone

attractions.name: Rio Grande Nature Center State Park
attractions.description: Rio Grande Nature Center State Park is a 270-acre urban wildlife preserve located on the Rio Grande flyway. It features indoor and outdoor wildlife viewing areas overlooking ponds, native plant gardens, and a pollinator garden, offering exceptional birdwatching opportunities throughout the year. Visitors can explore easy hiking trails through the bosque (riverside forest) or access the adjacent city-managed Paseo del Bosque trail. EMNRD, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Park Gates: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Daily); Visitor Center: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Daily); Nature Shop: Mon–Thu 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Fri–Sun 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM. EMNRD, Friends of RGNC

highlights: Exceptional birdwatching with over 250 species recorded on the Rio Grande flyway. Rio Grande Inn

Mariposaville Pollinator Garden and Native Plant Garden. EMNRD
Visitor Center with informative exhibits and the unique Friends Nature Shop. EMNRD
Live Rio Grande webcams for remote wildlife viewing. EMNRD
attractions.tips: The park is day-use only; no camping is permitted. Pets are only allowed in the parking area and on adjacent city trails, not within the state park grounds. attractions.name: Sandia Peak Aerial Tramway (Sandia Peak Tramway)
attractions.description: The Sandia Peak Tramway is an aerial tramway that transports visitors from the outskirts of Albuquerque to the 10,378-foot crest of the Sandia Mountains (Sandia Peak Tramway). It offers stunning 11,000 square mile panoramic views of the New Mexico landscape and provides access to numerous high-altitude hiking trails and seasonal skiing opportunities (Sandia Peak Tramway).

attractions.hours: Tram & Gift Shop: Daily 9:00 am – 8:00 pm (Uphill). Last passenger Tram down: Thursday–Monday at 10:00 pm; Tuesday–Wednesday at 9:00 pm (Sandia Peak Tramway).
attractions.admission: Round-Trip: Adult (21-61) $35, Young Adult (13-20) $30, Senior (62+) $30, Military $30, Child (2-12) $25, Child (Under 2) Free. One-Way: $24 for all tiers. A $3.70 grounds fee is added to each purchase (Sandia Peak Tramway).

Ascends 4,000 feet in a 15-minute scenic journey (Sandia Peak Tramway)

attractions.name: The Range Cafe
attractions.description: The Range Cafe is a beloved New Mexican institution known for its quirky, art-filled atmosphere and eclectic menu that blends diner classics with traditional Southwestern flavors. Originally established in 1992 in Bernalillo, the cafe has expanded to multiple locations across Albuquerque, maintaining a reputation for "ordinary food done extraordinarily well." Visitors can enjoy a wide variety of "from scratch" dishes, ranging from signature blue corn pancakes to green chile-infused favorites and award-winning homemade desserts. (Official Website)
attractions.address: 4401 Wyoming Blvd NE, Albuquerque, NM 87111 (Wyoming); 1050 Rio Grande Blvd NW, Albuquerque, NM 87104 (Rio Grande); 10019 Coors Blvd NW, Albuquerque, NM 87114 (Cottonwood)
attractions.region: Northeast Heights (Wyoming); Old Town/North Valley (Rio Grande)
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM, Thursday: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM, Friday: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM, Saturday: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM, Sunday: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM (Range Cafe Locations)
attractions.admission: Free admission; menu prices range from approximately $6.00 to $25.00 (Range Cafe Menu)
highlights: Blue Corn Pancakes with blueberries and toasted pinon
Breakfast Burrito served with green chile cheese hashbrowns
Green Chile Strips served with green chile ranch
NM Mac and Cheese with roasted green chile and Jack cheese sauce
Tom's Meatloaf with red wine mushroom gravy
Signature desserts including Death by Lemon and Mexican Latte Cake
attractions.tips: Ask for your chile "Christmas style" to experience both red and green varieties. Be sure to visit the on-site gift shop for eclectic local art and unique souvenirs. Note that Albuquerque locations primarily focus on breakfast and lunch service, while full dinner service is available at the original Bernalillo and Los Lunas locations. (Finding the Universe)

attractions.name: The Turquoise Museum The Turquoise Museum
attractions.description: The Turquoise Museum is a family-owned institution housed in the grand, French-inspired Gertrude Zachary Castle in downtown Albuquerque. It features one of the world's most extensive private collections of natural turquoise, showcasing the gemstone's history, science, and mining practices through elegant galleries and international specimens The Turquoise Museum, Only In Your State. Note: The museum galleries are currently in the process of moving to a new location, with limited availability for scheduled visits The Turquoise Museum.
attractions.address: 400 2nd St SW, Albuquerque, NM 87102 The Turquoise Museum, Visit Albuquerque
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM (by scheduled arrival); Sunday: Closed The Turquoise Museum, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Monday - Saturday The Turquoise Museum, Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Adults (18-55): $20; Seniors (55+), Children (0-17), Military (with ID), and AAA Members: $15; Children under 4: Free (with adult ticket) The Turquoise Museum, AAA Trip Canvas
highlights: The "Castle" experience in the former Gertrude Zachary residence Only In Your State
George Washington Stone, a massive turquoise piece resembling the profile of the first U.S. President Facebook
Educational exhibits on turquoise mining and the "Turquoise Group" of minerals The Turquoise Museum
Extensive collection of international turquoise jewelry from the Southwest, China, and Persia Lonely Planet
Self-guided video tours accessible via smartphone The Turquoise Museum
Historic displays of lapidary tools and the stone-cutting process The Turquoise Museum
attractions.tips: Advance reservations are required for scheduled tour times; visitors are encouraged to use their own smartphones and headphones for the museum's video tour. Parking at the site is free, and most guests spend between 1 and 4 hours exploring the galleries The Turquoise Museum, Room Escape Artist. Check the museum website for the latest move updates, as the physical galleries are expected to close in 2026 for a relocation KOB 4.

attractions.name: The University of New Mexico (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Founded in 1889, the University of New Mexico is the state's flagship research institution, celebrated for its unique Pueblo Revival architectural style. The 800-acre main campus in central Albuquerque serves as a cultural hub, featuring world-class museums, an arboretum, and the iconic Duck Pond (Wikipedia, UNM About).
attractions.hours: Campus Grounds: Open 24/7; Maxwell Museum: Tue-Sat 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Student Union Building: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Administrative Offices: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (UNM News, Maxwell Museum)
attractions.days_open: Monday–Sunday (Campus Grounds); Tuesday–Saturday (Major Museums) (Maxwell Museum, UNM News)
attractions.admission: Free; donations are encouraged at campus museums such as the Maxwell Museum of Anthropology (Maxwell Museum, UNM Museums).
highlights: Pueblo Revival Architecture throughout the campus (Wikipedia)
The Duck Pond, a serene relaxation spot for visitors (YouTube)
Maxwell Museum of Anthropology's renowned collections (UNM Events)
The University of New Mexico Arboretum with over 300 plant species (UNM Events)
Popejoy Hall, the premier venue for Broadway and musical events (UNM Events)
Historic Hodgin Hall, the university's first building (UNM Events)
attractions.tips: Visitors can enjoy self-guided tours of the campus arboretum and historic architecture. It is recommended to check individual museum hours before visiting and to use designated visitor parking lots or public transit, as on-campus parking is strictly enforced (UNM Admissions, UNM News).
attractions.nearby: The historic Nob Hill shopping and dining district, the iconic Frontier Restaurant, and the Route 66 Central Avenue corridor (Visit Albuquerque, Wikipedia).

attractions.description: Tractor Brewing Company has spent over two decades perfecting approachable craft beer in a social hub that combines award-winning brews with local culture. Their taprooms feature a rotation of 24 taps including house beers, ciders, and craft cocktails, often accompanied by live music, trivia, and rotating food trucks (Tractor Brewing Co.).

attractions.name: Valles Caldera National Preserve (NPS)
attractions.description: Valles Caldera National Preserve protects a spectacular 13.7-mile-wide volcanic caldera in the Jemez Mountains, formed by a massive eruption about 1.2 million years ago (Wikipedia). The landscape is characterized by vast high-altitude meadows, forested volcanic domes, and meandering streams that support diverse wildlife including a large elk population (NPS).
attractions.address: 39201 New Mexico Highway 4, Jemez Springs, NM 87025 (NPS)
attractions.hours: Summer (May 25 – September 7): 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Winter/Shoulder: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (NPS)
attractions.days_open: Daily, except for Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day (NPS)
attractions.admission: Private Vehicle: $25.00; Motorcycle: $20.00; Individual (walk-in/bicycle): $15.00; Annual Pass: $45.00 (NPS)
highlights: Wildlife viewing of elk, black bears, and prairie dogs (NPS)
Scenic driving through the massive Valle Grande meadow (NPS)
Extensive hiking trails ranging from gentle meadow walks to steep mountain climbs (NPS)
World-class stargazing in one of the darkest night sky locations (NPS)
Winter sports including cross-country skiing and snowshoeing (NPS)
Exploring volcanic geology such as lava domes and fumaroles (NPS)
attractions.tips: The preserve is at a high elevation (over 8,000 feet), so visitors should drink plenty of water to prevent altitude sickness (NPS). The park is cashless, and there is no potable water available on-site except for bottled water for purchase (NPS). Backcountry vehicle access is limited and requires a permit (NPS).
